memes.label: Beatrice Gilray
memes.description: A minor but telling character whose position as secretary and observer provides another angle on the novel's social landscape.

memes.label: Beauty
memes.description: Physical and aesthetic beauty as both ideal and trap in Point Counter Point — John Bidlake's pursuit of beautiful forms, the beauty of art versus the beauty of nature.

memes.label: British Freemen
memes.description: Everard Webley's uniformed political movement — a paramilitary nationalist organization that represents the authoritarian counterpoint in the novel's ideological structure.

memes.label: Chelsea
memes.description: The bohemian quarter where artists and intellectuals live — John Bidlake's studio and the world of art.

memes.label: Civilization
memes.description: Civilization as constraint and artifice in Point Counter Power — the 1920s cultural frame against which Huxley's characters define themselves.

memes.label: Class
memes.description: Social class as a determining force in characters' lives — the intersection of class with love, politics, and identity in 1920s England.

memes.label: Cuthbert Arkwright
memes.description: Cuthbert Arkwright is a publisher of expensive limited editions of scabrous literature, a noisy drunkard who justifies his excesses as a defence of art against philistinism, and a minor member of the Tantamount social circle.

memes.label: Cynicism and Idealism
memes.description: The oscillation between cynical disillusionment and idealistic aspiration that defines the novel's intellectual temperament — from Spandrell's nihilism to Quarles's analytical detachment.

memes.label: Death
memes.description: The pervasive presence of death in Point Counter Point — from Spandrell's murder to the metaphorical death of relationships, reflecting Huxley's preoccupation with mortality and meaning.

memes.label: Denis Burlap
memes.description: The sentimental literary editor whose spiritual posturing masks self-interest — religion degraded into emotional manipulation.

memes.label: Desire
memes.description: Desire as the driving force of character and plot in Point Counter Point — sexual, intellectual, and social longing as the engine of the novel's action.

memes.label: Dr. Crowther
memes.description: Dr. Crowther is the attending physician during little Phil Quarles's fatal illness, a brisk, telegraphically efficient doctor whose professional competence is inseparable from a chilling emotional economy.

memes.label: Elinor Quarles
memes.description: Philip Quarles's wife, whose companionate marriage represents the novel's most functional partnership — built on mutual respect and minimal emotional demand.

memes.label: Ethel Cobbett
memes.description: Ethel Cobbett is Denis Burlap's secretary at the *Literary World*, a passionately principled woman whose history of romantic disappointment and unwavering moral seriousness ultimately leads to her tragic suicide.

memes.label: Everard Webley
memes.description: Leader of the British Freemen, a fascist movement whose theatrical politics and violent end dramatize the novel's critique of ideological certainty.

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: The role of freedom in Point Counter Point — explored through character, plot, and the novel's contrapuntal structure.

memes.label: Gattenden
memes.description: Gattenden, the Tantamount country estate — a pastoral counterpoint to London's urban chaos, where the laboratory and the drawing room coexist in architectural and ideological tension.

memes.label: General Knoyle
memes.description: A military figure whose presence evokes the recent shadow of the Great War and the continuing claims of tradition and authority.

memes.label: Gladys Helmsley
memes.description: Gladys Helmsley is Sidney Quarles's working-class mistress, hired as his secretary-typist and drawn into a transactional affair in which she proves more perceptive and less reverent than her older lover expects.

memes.label: Hugo Brockle
memes.description: A young member of the British Freemen, admirer of Polly Logan, whose awkwardness and romantic interest place him at the intersection of the novel's political and emotional counterpoints (002-048).

memes.label: Illidge
memes.description: Frank Illidge, Lord Edward Tantamount's working-class laboratory assistant — a brilliant scientist whose class resentment fuels a political radicalism that the novel treats with both sympathy and irony.

memes.label: Intellectualism
memes.description: Intellectualism as a dominant yet self-defeating force in Point Counter Point — embodied in Philip Quarles's detachment and the novel's critique of pure reason.

memes.label: John Bidlake
memes.description: John Bidlake, the aging painter and patriarch whose philosophy of sensuality equates art with physical experience — a portrait of creative vitality confronting the indignities of age and illness.

memes.label: Knowledge
memes.description: The role of knowledge in Point Counter Point — explored through character, plot, and the novel's contrapuntal structure.

memes.label: Lady Edward Tantamount
memes.description: Hostess of the novel's central social gatherings, whose musical parties provide the stage on which the characters' dramas unfold.

memes.label: Lady Edward's Party
memes.description: Lady Edward Tantamount's musical parties — the novel's central social ritual where the contrapuntal method achieves its fullest expression through the simultaneous convergence of incompatible characters, philosophies, and desires.

memes.label: London
memes.description: 1920s London as the primary setting of Point Counter Point — a landscape of social stratification, emotional dislocation, and modern velocity that functions as both stage and character.

memes.label: Lord Edward Tantamount
memes.description: The aristocratic scientist absorbed in biological research — the novel's portrait of pure intellectual pursuit, indifferent to politics and emotion.

memes.label: Lord Gattenden
memes.description: Lord Gattenden, the fifth Marquess, is Lord Edward Tantamount's crippled elder brother, a reclusive philosopher who has spent fifty years pursuing metaphysical absolutes from a bath chair drawn by a grey donkey through Gattenden Park.

memes.label: Love
memes.description: The multifaceted portrayal of love in Point Counter Point — from romantic idealism to biological determinism, explored through parallel and contrasting relationships that form the novel's emotional counterpoint.

memes.label: Lucy Tantamount
memes.description: The self-possessed aristocrat who becomes the object of Walter Bidlake's obsessive infatuation — modernity embodied as feminine autonomy.

memes.label: Marjorie Carling
memes.description: Walter Bidlake's long-suffering partner whose anxious love and emotional dependence embody the novel's critique of possessive attachment.

memes.label: Mark Rampion
memes.description: The novel's Lawrentian prophet of vitality, who argues that civilization has been poisoned by over-reliance on intellect at the expense of instinct.

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: Marriage as institution and experience in Point Counter Point — from the Bidlakes' strained union to the Carling-Walter affair, reflecting the institution's crisis in the 1920s.

memes.label: Mary Rampion
memes.description: Mary Rampion is Mark Rampion's wife, a warm-hearted, physically vital woman who embodies her husband's philosophy of wholeness through lived experience rather than intellectual articulation.

memes.label: Maurice Spandrell
memes.description: The novel's dark center — a nihilist who murders Everard Webley and embraces his own destruction, embodying the death-drive that haunts the novel's intellectual world.

memes.label: Miss Fulkes
memes.description: Miss Fulkes is little Phil's governess at the Bidlake household, a young woman whose inner fantasy life of glamour and romance provides a poignant counterpoint to the mundane reality of her employment.

memes.label: Modernity
memes.description: The novel's portrait of 1920s modernity — motor cars, changing morals, political extremism, and the acceleration of life that leaves characters disoriented between tradition and the future.

memes.label: Molly d'Exergillod
memes.description: Molly d'Exergillod is a French comtesse and professional conversationalist whose obsessive transformation of experience into witty phrases makes her — in Philip Quarles's view — a "Max Beerbohm version of himself," a parody of consciousness that reveals the sterility of pure intellect.

memes.label: Morality
memes.description: Morality in Point Counter Point as class performance, biological accident, and counterpoint to desire — Huxley's anatomy of ethical life in 1920s England.

memes.label: Mr. Quarles (Sidney)
memes.description: Sidney Quarles, father of Philip Quarles, is a pompous political nonentity who retreats into an elaborate pretense of writing a magnum opus on democracy while concealing a hollow intellectual life.

memes.label: Mr. Sita Ram
memes.description: Mr. Sita Ram is an Indian nationalist politician who dines with Philip and Elinor Quarles in Bombay, a comic figure whose florid anti-colonial oratory and self-importance provide satirical counterpoint to the novel's other political characters.

memes.label: Mrs. Betterton
memes.description: Mary Betterton is a literary hostess in Lady Edward Tantamount's social circle, once a romantic interest of John Bidlake, now a stout and earnest middle-aged woman whose intellectual pretensions and moral earnestness make her a figure of gentle satire in the novel.

memes.label: Mrs. Knoyle
memes.description: Mrs. Knoyle is General Knoyle's wife and Maurice Spandrell's mother, a gentle, weak-willed woman trapped between her second husband's military rigidity and her son's destructive nihilism.

memes.label: Mrs. Logan
memes.description: Fanny Logan is Polly Logan's widowed mother, a sentimental and emotionally open woman whose grief for her dead husband and devotion to her daughter make her one of the novel's most warmly sympathetic minor characters.

memes.label: Music
memes.description: The role of music in Point Counter Point — explored through character, plot, and the novel's contrapuntal structure.

memes.label: Nature
memes.description: The natural world as a counterpoint to urban modernity in Point Counter Point — biological nature studied by science, vital nature celebrated by Rampion, and human nature dissected by Huxley's irony.

memes.label: Pall Mall
memes.description: The London club district representing tradition, male privilege, and the political establishment.

memes.label: Passion
memes.description: Passion as a driving force in Point Counter Point — from sexual desire to intellectual fervor, examined through characters who embody, resist, and theorize about the nature of intense feeling.

memes.label: Philip Quarles
memes.description: The novelist-within-the-novel whose analytical detachment serves as both intellectual method and emotional limitation — Huxley's self-critical portrait of the writer as observer.

memes.label: Politics
memes.description: Political ideology and extremism in Point Counter Point — from Webley's British Freemen to parliamentary liberalism, reflecting the crisis of 1920s political order.

memes.label: Polly Logan
memes.description: Polly Logan is Fanny Logan's twenty-year-old daughter, a vivacious and theatrically self-conscious young woman who moves through Lady Edward Tantamount's social circle with the enthusiastic naivety of youth.

memes.label: Rachel Quarles
memes.description: Rachel Quarles is Philip's mother and Sidney Quarles's long-suffering wife — a quietly efficient, ardently religious woman whose competence and faith form a silent rebuke to her husband's pomposity.

memes.label: Reason and Emotion
memes.description: The central contrapuntal pair of Point Counter Point — reason versus emotion as the novel's structural principle, embodied across every character and relationship.

memes.label: Religion
memes.description: Religious faith and its discontents in Point Counter Point — from Burlap's sentimental mysticism to Spandrell's nihilistic anti-religion, reflecting the 1920s crisis of belief.

memes.label: Sbisa
memes.description: A Soho restaurant where characters meet for intellectual exchange — the novel's convivial space.

memes.label: Science and Art
memes.description: The novel's contrapuntal opposition between scientific rationalism and artistic sensibility, embodied in characters who champion biology over beauty or vice versa.

memes.label: Sidney Quarles
memes.description: Sidney Quarles is Philip Quarles's father, a pompous, ineffectual former politician and would-be author of "the largest book on democracy ever written" — a man whose entire life is a façade concealing mediocrity.

memes.label: Spandrell's Murder of Webley
memes.description: The novel's violent climax — Maurice Spandrell's assassination of Everard Webley, the moment when contrapuntal debates about politics and meaning become irreversible action, transforming the novel's intellectual structure.

memes.label: Walter Bidlake
memes.description: A young journalist trapped between his declining relationship with Marjorie Carling and his obsessive infatuation with Lucy Tantamount — the novel's central consciousness of emotional failure.

memes.label: Walter-Lucy Affair
memes.description: Walter Bidlake's obsessive pursuit of Lucy Tantamount — the novel's central romantic drama and a study in the asymmetry of desire.
